channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Hotel in Iraqi capital Baghdad struck as attacks on US embassy intercepted
item.description: No group has claimed responsibility for the attacks, which took place amid the escalating Israel-US war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: Russia has taken 12 settlements in Ukraine as part of general advances, top general says
item.description: MOSCOW, March 16 (Reuters) - Russia has taken control of 12 settlements in Ukraine in the first two weeks of March as part of advances along the front line in eastern and southern Ukraine, Russian

item.title: Rubio designates Iranian-American Kamran Hekmati as wrongfully detained by Iran, cousin says
item.description: WASHINGTON, March 16 (Reuters) - Secretary of State Marco Rubio has designated as wrongfully detained in Iran 61-year-old Jewish Iranian-American Kamran Hekmati, his cousin said on Monday.

item.title: Multiple suicide bombers hit Nigeria's Maiduguri city after years of calm
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: Number of US troops wounded in war against Iran rises to about 200
item.description: By Phil Stewart and Idrees Ali WASHINGTON, March 16 (Reuters) - The number of U.S. troops wounded in the war against Iran has risen to about 200, the U.S. military said on Monday, as the conflict

item.title: Afghanistan says more than 200 people were killed in a strike by Pakistan on a Kabul hospital treating drug addicts
item.description: Afghanistan says more than 200 people were killed in a strike by Pakistan on a Kabul hospital treating drug addicts.

item.title: Cuba’s power grid collapses after weeks of US oil blockade
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid suffered a total collapse on Monday, the country’s power operator said, marking the latest nationwide blackout in recent years, and the first since the US effectively shut off the flow of oil to the island of roughly 10 million people.

item.title: Bombs explode in northeastern Nigeria, leaving scores killed and injured, authorities say
item.description: Bombs exploded in at least three locations in northeastern Nigeria’s Borno state Monday night, killing and injuring scores of people, the emergency services told The Associated Press, citing possible suicide bombings. Explosions were heard in Maiduguri, the Borno state capital, where Nigeria’s homegrown jihadi Boko Haram extremists have waged an insurgency for more than a decade.

item.title: Cuba electric grid collapses amid US oil blockade causing national blackout
item.description: Cuba has not received oil shipments since early January amid US pressure, worsening humanitarian crisis on the island.

item.title: Israel president tells AFP Europe should back efforts to 'eradicate' Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog told AFP Monday that Europe should back his country's fight against Hezbollah, as Israeli forces carried out ground operations in Lebanon.Israel's military announced Monday it was conducting "limited" ground operations against Iran-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon to end its rocket attacks.

item.title: Canada, France, Germany, UK warn against major Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: By Kanishka Singh WASHINGTON, March 16 (Reuters) - A "significant Israeli ground offensive" in Lebanon must be averted since it could have devastating humanitarian consequences, Canada, France,

item.title: Factbox-US Health Secretary Kennedy's vaccine actions and policy positions
item.description: judge on Monday blocked key parts of Health Secretary Robert F. vaccine policy, including a move to reduce the number of shots routinely recommended for children.

item.title: Peter Thiel’s secret lectures on Antichrist in Rome spark debate
item.description: Billionaire venture capitalist Peter Thiel has previously argued that the Antichrist is not necessarily a person but could come as a global government system.

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments over push to end legal protections for migrants from Haiti, Syria
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments over the Trump administration’s push to end legal protections for people fleeing war and natural disaster from countries around the world, including Haiti and Syria. The Trump administration filed emergency appeals after lower courts stopped the immediate end of the program for 350,000 people from Haiti and 6,000 people from Syria. The administration asked the court to lift those decisions and issue a broad ruling that would block courts from intervening when Homeland Security decides to end protections.

item.title: Alleged narco trafficker makes first US court appearance
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
item.description: Here are the latest developments Monday in the Middle East war: - Baghdad hotel attack - A fire broke out late Monday on the roof of a hotel in Baghdad's Green Zone, witnesses told AFP, with the interior ministry saying there were no casualties or damage after a "projectile" landed on the building.- Drone targets Iraqi oil field - Two drones targeted a major southern Iraqi oil field late Monday, an oil ministry spokesperson told AFP, after the second attack in four days.

item.title: Migrant workers bear brunt of Iran attacks in Gulf
item.description: Bangladeshi migrant Ahmad Ali, 55, was doing his regular round delivering drinking water to residents in the United Arab Emirates when Iran launched its first retaliatory attacks against Gulf countries.Since February 28, Tehran has launched wave after wave of missile and drone attacks against the Gulf states in retaliation for US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

item.title: Mediterranean states say adrift Russian tanker poses 'imminent and serious' threat
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: South Lebanon's Christian towns insist they are not part of Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: In southern Lebanon's Ain Ebel, close to the border with Israel, Suad Jallad holds a poster of her son, killed by Israel last week, saying she would rather be buried next to him than leave.He'd say, 'I won't leave Ain Ebel,'" Jallad said.

item.title: Securing the Strait of Hormuz: Tactics and threats
item.description: President Donald Trump is pressuring allies to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime choke point that has effectively been closed by Iran in response to the US-Israeli war launched last month.Trump added that Washington did not know if any mines had actually been placed in the Strait of Hormuz by Iran.

item.title: Cuba hit by total blackout as US fuel blockade bites
item.description: A power outage struck the entire island of Cuba on Monday, the state-owned electric company said, the latest blackout as the United States pursues its oil blockade against the island's communist government.Trump has alleged the fuel blockade is a response to an "extraordinary threat" posed by Cuba to the United States.

item.title: Iranian families weep as war dead are buried in Tehran cemetery
item.description: Marzia Rezaei wept for her son Erfan Shamei, who died in a blast at a military training camp days before he was due home on leave.

item.title: Cuba's national electric grid collapses, leaving millions without power
item.description: By Dave Sherwood HAVANA, March 16 (Reuters) - Cuba's national electric grid collapsed on Monday, the country's grid operator said, leaving around 10 million people without power amid a U.S.-imposed

item.title: Cuba's national energy grid collapses sparking nationwide blackout
item.description: The latest blackout in Cuba comes over a week after another massive outage​ affected the island's west, leaving millions without power.

item.title: Factbox-How many people have been killed in the US-Israeli war on Iran?
item.description: March 16 (Reuters) - At least 2,000 people have been killed across the Middle East since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, with Gulf states hosting U.S. military bases and personnel,

item.title: Photos from the Mideast in the 3rd week of the Iran war
item.description: Photos from the ground as the war on Iran launched by the United States and Israel enters its third week. This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

item.title: Cuba’s power system suffers total collapse
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid suffered a total collapse on Monday, the country’s power operator said, marking the latest nationwide blackout in recent years, and the first since the US effectively shut off the flow of oil to Cuba.

item.title: New terror group with reported Iran ties claims 4 attacks across Europe
item.description: Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamiyya attacks on European Jewish institutions may be linked to Iranian proxy networks, according to security analysts and Israeli officials.

item.title: Iranian families weep as war dead are buried in Tehran cemetery
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Brazil's Bolsonaro transferred to semi-intensive care unit after improvement, wife says
item.description: SAO PAULO, March 16 (Reuters) - Brazil's former President Jair Bolsonaro has been transferred to a semi-intensive care unit after showing improvement in inflammatory markers as he continues treatment

item.title: Outrage as Afghan asylum seeker who fought alongside US dies in ICE custody
item.description: Family, advocates call for full probe after Mohommad Nazeer Paktyawal dies less than 24 hours after being detained.

item.title: Exclusive-US SEC enforcement director to leave agency after months on job, sources say
item.description: By Dawn Kopecki and Chris Prentice NEW YORK, March 16 (Reuters) - The U.S.

item.title: Afghan govt accuses Pakistan after new strikes on Kabul
item.description: Afghan authorities on Monday accused neighbouring Pakistan of attacking Kabul, as AFP journalists heard loud explosions overhead and anti-aircraft defences swung into action.There was no comment either from the Pakistani military when contacted by AFP. The sound of anti-aircraft guns stopped at about 10:00 pm and ambulance sirens could be heard.

item.title: Cuban officials report an island-wide blackout as country struggles with energy crisis
item.description: Officials in Cuba reported an island-wide blackout Monday in the country of some 11 million people as its energy and economic crises deepen. Cuba has blamed its woes on a U.S. energy blockade after President Donald Trump in January warned of tariffs on any country that sells or provides oil to it. The Ministry of Energy and Mines on X noted a “complete disconnection” of the country’s electrical system and said it was investigating.

item.title: Lebanon says more than 1 million people have been displaced since latest fighting between Israel and Hezbollah began
item.description: Lebanon says more than 1 million people have been displaced since latest fighting between Israel and Hezbollah began.

item.title: ‘Ticking time bomb’: Stricken Russian fuel tanker drifts near to Italian islands
item.description: Maritime authorities have warned mariners to steer clear of a crewless Russian fuel tanker that has been drifting between Italy and Malta for days after an alleged drone attack, prompting fears of an environmental disaster.

item.title: Czech president adds to criticism of government's defence spending cuts
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: US responsible for deadly attack on Iranian school: Amnesty International
item.description: Rights group says Tomahawk missile likely used in Minab school strike that killed at least 170 people, mostly children.

item.title: Afghanistan says children among four killed in latest attacks by Pakistan
item.description: The civilians – three children and a woman – are killed in an attack on Khost province, local official says.

item.title: Peruvian left-wing candidate gaining ground in presidential race, poll shows
item.description: (Corrects name of Lopez Chau's party in paragraph 2 to Ahora Nacion, not Juntos por el Peru) LIMA, March 16 (Reuters) - Peruvian left-wing candidate Alfonso Lopez Chau is closing in on the two leading

item.title: With the US pressuring Cuba, a Castro known as ‘the Crab’ is making appearances. Here’s why it matters
item.description: Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro, grandson of former Cuban President Raúl Castro, appeared in several public events led by Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel on Friday, raising questions, according to analysts, about his role in Cuba’s leadership as the island faces calls for regime change from the United States.

item.title: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has breast cancer but will keep working through treatment
item.description: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with early stage breast cancer but plans to continue working through her treatment, retaining her place as one of President Donald Trump's closest aides during a period of political turbulence. Wiles, 68, announced on Monday that she had been diagnosed over the previous week. “Nearly one in eight women in the United States will face this diagnosis,” said Wiles, who's the first woman to hold her position.

item.title: 2 dead, including girl, after boats carrying students capsize off Japan
item.description: Coast guard rescuers pulled all 21 people out of the water, but a 17-year-old student and the captain of one of the boats were later pronounced dead.

item.title: Why Iranian drones are hard to stop
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: Hours-long fuel queues in Laos capital Vientiane
item.description: Long queues formed at petrol stations across the Laotian capital Vientiane on Monday as fuel shortages deepened, with the ripples of the Middle East conflict reverberating across landlocked Laos.AFP saw more than 15 petrol stations shuttered in Vientiane on Monday, with signs saying they had run out of fuel.

item.title: Will Yemen's Houthis join the Mideast war?
item.description: With assault rifles, daggers and posters of Iran's late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei waving in the air as tens of thousands chant "death to America, death to Israel", there's little mistaking where the loyalties of Yemen's Houthis lie. The group was also battered by rounds of US and Israeli air strikes after starting their Red Sea campaign in October 2023.

item.title: Kenya arrests man for smuggling live ants
item.description: Kenyan prosecutors say ant smuggling is a major problem.

item.title: Rise of drone warfare sharpens focus on laser defense
item.description: The surge of drone use in conflicts worldwide, seen most vividly in the Ukraine and Middle East wars, will accelerate the race to develop high-power laser systems that could down the devices far more cheaply than traditional defensive weapons.At that price, not even Iran's notorious Shahed drones, estimated to cost as low as $20,000 each, or drone interceptors developed by Ukraine, whose costs start at around $700, can compete.

item.title: Pope Leo meets with Gareth Gore, writer who wants Opus Dei investigation
item.description: It's rare for Pope Leo to meet individually with a journalist. Gore has called for an investigation into the Catholic organization Opus Dei.

item.title: France threatens to block funds for India over climate inaction
item.description: France warned Monday it could block the disbursement of climate funds to India under a recent trade deal with the EU if New Delhi does not up its game in fighting global warming.As an example, she singled out India, which signed a major trade deal with Brussels in January.

item.title: Pakistan-bound oil tanker passes through Hormuz Strait amid Iran war
item.description: By Ariba Shahid KARACHI, March 16 (Reuters) - Ship-tracking data shows a Pakistan-bound oil tanker passing through the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, indicating that some countries are able to

item.title: UN Afghan mission gets limited extension after US review call
item.description: March 16 (Reuters) - The U.N.

item.title: Qatar Airways announces ‘limited’ flights to and from Doha
item.description: Airline says limited number of flights will operate as of March 18 between Qatari capital and dozens of destinations.

item.title: Nepal welcomes first transgender lawmaker
item.description: Draped in garlands, Bhumika Shrestha on Monday became Nepal's first transgender woman lawmaker, marking a proud milestone for the marginalised community in the Himalayan nation.But no one from the community has held public office since 2008, when an openly gay man became a lawmaker, nominated under the proportional representation system.

item.title: Hotels, flats turn away Lebanese displaced over fears they could be Israeli targets
item.description: After being forced to flee with his family from Israeli air raids on southern Lebanon, Hussein Fakih searched in vain for an apartment, amid strict measures imposed by hotels and landlords afraid that guests might be targeted by Israel.Since then, Israel has been conducting air raids across Lebanon and ground incursions into the border region.

item.title: Paris talks with Chinese were 'constructive', says US Treasury secretary
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Monday that talks with top Chinese officials in the French capital had been "constructive". Bessent met Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng, with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer also attending the talks, according to his office.

item.title: Kenya and Russia agree no Kenyans will be recruited for Ukraine war
item.description: Kenyan foreign minister meets Russian counterpart amid reports that hundreds of Kenyans are fighting in Russia, Ukraine.

item.title: US says it has destroyed Iran missile capacity: How is Iran still shooting?
item.description: Despite degraded launch capabilities, Iran has enough missiles to fire strategically, keep region on edge, say experts.

item.title: Qatar calls on Iran to stop Gulf attacks, urges diplomatic solution
item.description: Qatar foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari accuses Iran of targeting civilian areas as he calls for de-escalation.

item.title: Afghanistan says Pakistani mortars killed 2 children as fighting stretches into third week
item.description: Afghanistan’s Taliban forces and Pakistan’s military exchanged fire at multiple locations along their shared border, killing at least two children and wounding 10 people in southeastern Afghanistan, Afghan officials said Monday, as fighting continued for a third consecutive week despite international calls for a ceasefire. Ten people were also injured when mortar shells fired from Pakistan overnight struck villages in Afghanistan’s Khost province, said Mustaghfar Gurbaz, a spokesman for the provincial governor. The latest violence came a day after Pakistan said a mortar fired from Afghanistan hit a house in the northwestern Bajaur district, killing four members of the same family and wounding two others, including a 5-year-old child.

item.title: South Africa rejects US pressure to distance itself from Iran
item.description: By Tim Cocks JOHANNSESBURG, March 16 (Reuters) - South Africa has no reason to cut ties with Iran, its director general of foreign affairs said, after the new U.S. ambassador was quoted as saying the

item.title: US warns Iraq must act against Iran-backed militia attacks on American assets
item.description: Iran-backed Popular Mobilization Forces have been accused of attacking U.S. assets in Iraq as Prime Minister Mohammed Shia al-Sudani's government told to rein in terror proxies.

item.title: Iran, at UN, insists will not submit to 'lawless aggression'
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: EU sanctions Chinese and Iranian companies for cyber attacks
item.description: The European Union on Monday imposed sanctions against ‌two China-based and one Iranian ‌company for cyber attacks against EU member ​states. The EU listed China-based Integrity Technology Group and Anxun Information Technology, and Iranian company Emennet Pasargad. * Anxun is seen to have provided ​hacking services ​aimed atcritical ​infrastructure, according to ‌the EU.

item.title: Appeal trial opens for France's Sarkozy over alleged Libyan funding
item.description: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy was back in court Monday for a retrial on charges he sought Libyan financing for his 2007 election, in a case that last year saw him become France's first modern-day head of state to go to prison.In the so-called "Libyan case", he has appealed a five-year prison sentence.

item.title: Kenyans will no longer be enlisted to fight for Russia in Ukraine, minister says
item.description: By Guy Faulconbridge MOSCOW, March 16 (Reuters) - Kenya said on Monday that it had agreed with Russia that Kenyans would no longer be eligible for signing up to fight in the war with Ukraine after the

item.title: Israel army says begun 'limited targeted ground operations' against Hezbollah in south Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said on Monday it had begun what it described as "limited ground operations" against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, with Defence Minister Israel Katz warning that those displaced would not return home until northern Israel was secure.- No evacuations from north Israel - Katz warned on Monday that hundreds of thousands of Lebanese displaced by fighting with Hezbollah would not be allowed to return home "south of the Litani area until the safety of residents in the north (of I

item.title: The US is considering Hormuz naval escorts. There are risks and it could go disastrously wrong
item.description: As US President Donald Trump pressures American allies – and even China – to contribute naval forces to protect shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, naval experts say such an endeavor presents a huge risk that, even if successful, might restore only about 10% of the pre-war traffic through the waterway.

item.title: Israel says it has plans for at least three weeks of war as airstrikes pound Iran
item.description: By Alexander Cornwell and Parisa Hafezi TEL AVIV/DUBAI, March 16 (Reuters) - Israel said on Monday it has detailed plans for at least three more weeks of war as its military pounded sites across Iran

item.title: UK's Starmer resists being drawn into wider Iran war, offers help on strait
item.description: By Elizabeth Piper and Sarah Young LONDON, March 15 (Reuters) - Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday Britain would not be drawn into a wider war in Iran but would work with allies on a "viable"

item.title: Strait of Hormuz: Which countries’ ships has Iran allowed safe passage to?
item.description: Tehran allows a small number of ships from certain countries to pass through waterway.

item.title: Spain rules out participating in military operations in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: MADRID, March 16 (Reuters) - Spain will not take part in any military mission in the Strait of Hormuz because it considers the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran to be illegal, Madrid's defence and foreign

item.title: Live updates: Israel carries out new wave of ‘extensive’ airstrikes on Iran
item.description: Israel's military said the strikes are targeting the Iranian regime's infrastructure and military sites.

item.title: Unclear timeline for Iran war keeps high gas prices in limbo
item.description: Lowered energy costs could come after the fighting abroad is finished, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said over the weekend.

item.title: Israelis flock to desert resort to escape stress of war
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: Quran echoes loudly as Palestinian reciters gather in Gaza
item.description: Quran memorisers in Gaza overcome devastation from war, reciting the holy book as a source of hope and strength.

item.title: Israel says Lebanese displaced won't return until its own citizens are safe
item.description: By Alexander Cornwell and Maya Gebeily TEL AVIV/BEIRUT, March 16 (Reuters) - Israel on Monday warned that displaced Lebanese driven from their homes by its military would not be able to return until

item.title: Pope Leo asks media to show suffering of war, not amplify 'propaganda'
item.description: By Joshua McElwee VATICAN CITY, March 16 (Reuters) - Pope Leo urged journalists on Monday to highlight the suffering caused by war, cautioning against news reports that risk sliding into propaganda by

item.title: Race for Paris mayor on knife's edge after first round
item.description: The race for Paris mayor looked uncertain Monday as a right-wing former minister hoping to wrest control of the French capital from the left claimed she had gained key support for a second-round run-off.Rachida Dati, until recently France's culture minister, hopes to become the capital's second woman mayor in a row, and bring Paris under control of the right for the first time in 25 years.

item.title: UK, allies working on 'viable' plan for Hormuz but no NATO mission: PM
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday Britain was working with allies to come up with a "viable" plan to reopen the key Strait of Hormuz, but ruled out a NATO mission."Let me be clear, that won't be and it's never been envisioned to be a NATO mission," Starmer said.

item.title: Roof collapse at Pakistan welfare payment site kills at least 8 women
item.description: The roof of a shop collapsed as women in an eastern Pakistani village gathered Monday to collect government welfare payments, killing at least eight and injuring more than 50, police and rescue officials said. The roof gave way under the weight of the crowd, after the shopkeeper asked some of more than 100 women to move onto the roof while others remained inside the shop, rescuer Ashiq Mahmood said. The Benazir Income Support Program, named after former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto, who was assassinated in a 2007 gun and bomb attack, provides cash assistance to millions of low-income families, many of them women.

item.title: Frenchman jailed in Azerbaijan for 10 years for 'spying'
item.description: Azerbaijan on Monday sentenced a Frenchman to 10 years in prison in an espionage case launched during a period of diplomatic tensions between Paris and Baku that have now eased.The court found him guilty of spying against Azerbaijan and sentenced him to 10 years, an AFP reporter in the courtroom said.

item.title: Artillery attack on a Yemen village kills at least 10 as government blames Houthi rebels
item.description: An artillery attack on a village in northern Yemen killed at least 10 people, including six children, and wounded more than 30 others, officials said Monday, while the government blamed Iran-backed Houthi rebels for the shelling. The Houthis targeted a group of people gathered for iftar, the evening meal that breaks the daily fast during the holy month of Ramadan, in Hajjah province, according to the Information Ministry. The province is mostly controlled by the Houthis, but some areas are held by Yemen’s internationally recognized government that is based in Aden.

item.title: Middle Eastern Politics Headlines at 11:56 a.m. GMT
item.description: AP finds an Israeli group discreetly organized the mystery flights evacuating Palestinians from Gaza

item.title: Oil loading resumes at UAE's Fujairah port after an attack, sources say
item.description: DUBAI, March 16 (Reuters) - Oil loading operations have resumed at the United Arab Emirates port of Fujairah, two sources told Reuters on Monday, after it was halted earlier following a drone attack

item.title: EU weighs action to keep Strait of Hormuz open
item.description: The European Union is seeking more strategic clarity about the U.S. and Israel’s plans for Iran and when the conflict might end as the bloc weighs whether to send ships to help shore up security in the Persian Gulf. “It is in our interest to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, and that’s why we are also discussing what we can do in this regard from the European side,” Kaja Kallas, the EU’s foreign policy chief, said Monday ahead of a meeting of the 27-nation bloc’s foreign ministers in Brussels. U.S. President Donald Trump has asked allies — including France, China, Japan, South Korea and Britain — to help secure the strait for global shipping.

item.title: Tricky negotiations begin Monday to renew a trade pact between the United States, Mexico and Canada
item.description: Every day more than $4 billion worth of goods cross the United States’ borders with Canada and Mexico – U.S. auto parts headed for car factories in northern Mexico, cartons of Mexican avocados bound for California supermarkets, Canadian aluminum destined to become cans of Campbell Soup. Much of this bustling cross-border commerce is duty-free, thanks to the US-Mexico-Canada Agreement, or USMCA, that President Donald Trump negotiated with America’s northern and southern neighbors during his first term. The United States is demanding changes to the treaty, and the top U.S. trade negotiator told Politico in December that Trump would be willing to pull the United States out of the pact if he can’t get the deal he wants.

item.title: Azerbaijan jails Frenchman for a decade in espionage case
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz paralyzed and oil prices over $100
item.description: Source: CBS News

item.title: Fire at India hospital intensive care unit kills 10 people
item.description: Fire in the city of Cuttack likely caused by a short circuit, which is common in India due to poorly maintained wiring.

item.title: EU to impose sanctions on nine people for alleged war crimes in Ukraine, says French minister
item.description: BRUSSELS, March 16 (Reuters) - French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said on Monday that the European Union would impose sanctions on nine people accused of war crimes related to the Bucha massacre

item.title: Israeli military launches ‘ground operations’ in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli army says its forces are targeting key locations in southern Lebanon, aiming to expand forward defence zones.

item.title: Middle East war: global economic fallout
item.description: Here are the latest economic events in the Middle East war on Monday: - Oil rises, stocks fall - Oil prices rose further above $100 a barrel and Asian stocks mostly fell as the Iran war moved into a third week.IEA members agreed on March 11 to tap oil stockpiles to cushion the surge in prices caused by the war -- by far the largest-ever response of its kind.

item.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
item.description: Here are the latest events in the Middle East war: - Blasts in Tehran - Explosions hit the Iranian capital on Monday, an AFP journalist reported, hours after Israel said it conducted large-scale overnight strikes. The blasts were heard in central Tehran as air defence systems were activated, the journalist said.

item.title: Map shows how 16 days of attacks have evolved in US-Israel war on Iran
item.description: Here’s a day-by-day breakdown of attacks, illustrating the targets hit and the weapons used since February 28.

item.title: 2 dead after 2 boats carrying students capsize off US base construction site in southern Japan
item.description: Two boats carrying 21 people capsized Monday off Henoko, a controversial relocation site for a U.S. military base off Japan's southern island of Okinawa, throwing all into the water and leaving two of them dead, officials said. The Japan Coast Guard said 18 of them were students from a Kyoto high school on two boats, 10 on Heiwa Maru and eight on the smaller Fukutsu, to observe the Henoko area as part of their peace education program. Coast guard rescuers pulled all 21 people out of the water, but a 17-year-old female student and the captain of Fukutsu were later pronounced dead, officials said.

item.title: Indonesia police seek four suspects for acid attack on activist
item.description: Indonesian police are hunting for four people wanted for an acid attack that maimed an activist critical of the military's growing role in government, an official said Monday.Andrie has been a vocal critic of moves to increase the influence of the military in the Indonesian government and had just finished recording a podcast on the topic when he was attacked.

item.title: Kazakhstan's new constitution cementing president's grip on power approved in a referendum
item.description: Source: Associated Press

item.title: EU's Kallas floats Black Sea model to unblock Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: 5th member of Iran women's soccer team changes mind on asylum in Australia
item.description: A fifth of seven members of the Iran women's soccer team who accepted refugee visas to stay in Australia has changed her mind, a sport official says.

item.title: Blasts rock Kyiv in rare daytime Russian attack: AFP
item.description: Explosions rang out over the Ukrainian capital on Monday and AFP journalists witnessed air defence batteries fending off a barrage of Russian projectiles during a rare daytime attack on Kyiv.The Russian capital has also been targeted by Ukrainian drones over recent days, Moscow mayor Sergei Sobyanin said, adding that Russian air defence systems had downed around 250 drones "as they approached Moscow."

item.title: Israel announces Lebanon ground assault against Hezbollah
item.description: Israel said Monday it had launched ground operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon while US President Donald Trump piled pressure on world powers to help reopen the vital shipping lane choked off by Iranian attacks.Israel also announced the launch of "limited ground operations" against Iran-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, a key front in the wider war.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 17 of US-Israel attacks?
item.description: Dubai contained a fire near its airport and temporarily suspended flights, while Trump claimed Iran wants to negotiate.

item.title: Drone strike disrupts Dubai flights as Iran continues Gulf attacks
item.description: At least one person killed in Abu Dhabi, Dubai flights disrupted and fire breaks out in Fujairah after Iranian attacks.

item.title: 'Rein in your heart': I.Coast women trapped by divorce taboo
item.description: Josy's life was turned upside down three years ago when she discovered that her husband had racked up crippling debts and was cheating on her. "I really want a divorce but I can't afford it," said Nina, 40, whose husband left five years ago with three of their four children.

item.title: Iranian protesters fled 'hell' at home, watch war from exile
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: Madagascar names anticorruption chief as PM days after cabinet dissolved
item.description: President announces Mamitiana Rajaonarison as prime minister after Herintsalama Rajaonarivelo was dismissed.

item.title: French jihadist presumed dead to be tried for Yazidi 'genocide'
item.description: A French jihadist presumed dead is to be tried in absentia Monday accused of "genocide" in the first such trial in France over Islamic State group crimes against the Yazidi minority in the Middle East."Given that in the past Islamic State fighters believed to be dead have resurfaced, it is essential that this trial take place," said Patrick Baudouin, a lawyer for the Human Rights League, a French rights organisation.

item.title: Exclusive-US 'pro-family' group worked with Senegal activists pushing anti-LGBT law
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Philippines rejects Beijing's claim to sovereignty over South China Sea
item.description: MANILA, March 16 (Reuters) - The Philippine foreign ministry said on Monday it rejected Beijing's assertion of jurisdiction over the entire South China Sea, asserting what it called Manila's "

item.title: Analysis-Middle East war disrupts pharma air routes, risks cancer drugs supply
item.description: LONDON, March 16 () - War in the Middle East is disrupting the flow of critical medicines to the Gulf, imperilling supply routes for cancer drugs and other treatments that require refrigeration and forcing companies to reroute flights and find overland access into the region, industry executives said. and Israeli attacks on Iran ‌two weeks ago and broadened by Iranian strikes around the region, has knocked out key air transit hubs and closed shipping routes, snarling the movement of goods for ma

item.title: Israeli forces kill 16 people in Gaza and the West Bank, medics say
item.description: By Nidal al-Mughrabi and Ali Sawafta CAIRO/RAMALLAH, March 15 (Reuters) - Israeli forces killed 16 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, health officials said, in one of the heaviest death

item.title: Rafah crossing closure leaves Gaza patients trapped without treatment
item.description: Thousands face worsening illness after closure of key crossing halts medical evacuations.

item.title: Fifth member of Iran women's soccer team withdraws asylum claim
item.description: SYDNEY/KUALA LUMPUR, March 16 () - A fifth member of the Iranian women's soccer team has withdrawn her claim for asylum in Australia, Australian media reported on Monday, and will be rejoining ‌the rest of the squad in Malaysia. The latest withdrawal leaves only two people of the ‌original six players and one support staff member who sought asylum in Australia last week fearing possible persecution if they returned home.

item.title: Russia says Ukraine struck Moscow with over 100 drones this weekend
item.description: MOSCOW, March 16 (Reuters) - Russia said on Monday that Ukraine launched a major drone attack on Moscow with more than 100 drones over the weekend, with wave after wave of long-range "kamikaze" drones

item.title: Iran hits Gulf neighbors and keeps stranglehold on oil shipping as concerns rise of energy crisis
item.description: Explosions echoed across Beirut early Monday as Israel struck the Lebanese capital. Since being attacked by the United States and Israel more than two weeks ago, Iran has been regularly hitting Israel, American bases and its Gulf Arab neighbors' energy infrastructure with drones and missiles. It has also effectively stopped shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world's oil is transported, giving rise to growing fears of a global energy crisis and putting pressure on Washington as consumers are already feeling the pain at the pump.

item.title: Iran war an 'abject lesson' on fossil fuel dependence, UN climate chief says
item.description: By Kate Abnett BRUSSELS, March 16 (Reuters) - The disruption to energy markets caused by the Iran war is an "abject lesson" in the risks of relying on fossil fuels, and underscores the case for

item.title: A harsh climate for many LGBTQ people in Africa is getting worse. Some blame the US
item.description: When 22-year-old Wendy Faith shared a Valentine’s Day kiss with 21-year-old Alesi Diana Denise in Uganda — a country infamous for enforcing some of the world’s strictest anti-LGBTQ laws — the lives of both women were about to take a complicated turn.

item.title: Fire near Dubai airport under control after drone attack, flights suspended
item.description: (Corrects to Gulf countries as well as Iraq, instead of such as Iraq, in paragraph 4) By Yomna Ehab March 16 (Reuters) - Dubai authorities brought under control on Monday a fire caused by a drone

item.title: What we know on the 17th day of the US and Israel’s war with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump is trying to garner international support for securing the Strait of Hormuz – the vital waterway where the transport of oil has shuddered to a halt since the US and Israel began their war with Iran.

item.title: Myanmar’s parliament meets for first time in 5 years with military controlling most seats
item.description: Myanmar opened its first parliamentary session in more than five years on Monday following an election that did not include major opposition parties, ensuring that the ruling military is set to retain a firm grasp on power. The military blocked Myanmar's last parliament from convening when it seized power from the last legitimately elected government of Aung San Suu Kyi in February 2021, and has governed without a legislature since then.

item.title: Why France is bolstering military presence in the Middle East, as Macron prepares for postwar talks
item.description: France's government is bolstering the country's military presence in the Middle East, dispatching its aircraft carrier and other warships, while President Emmanuel Macron engages with key players in the conflict, including Iran, in a bid to position Paris for future diplomatic talks. Macron said that France’s military involvement is strictly “defensive” and aims to avoid making the country a party to the war.

item.title: U.S. beats Dominican Republic 2-1 to reach 3rd straight WBC title game
item.description: Team USA's celebration after beating the Dominican Republic on Sunday night in the World Baseball Classic semifinals was electric and brief. NL Cy Young award winner Paul Skenes roared “Let's go!” after the final out. Closer Mason Miller let out a yell and pointed toward the sky.

item.title: Paul Thomas Anderson wins best director Oscar for ‘One Battle After Another’
item.description: Source: Associated Press

item.title: Family drama ‘Sentimental Value’ wins best international film Oscar, the first win for Norway
item.description: Source: Associated Press

item.title: France's Macron urges Iran to cease regional attacks, restore Hormuz navigation
item.description: March 15 () - French President Emmanuel Macron said on Sunday he had asked ‌Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to put an ‌immediate end to attacks against countries in the Middle ​East whether directly or through proxies, including in Lebanon and Iraq. "I reminded him that France is acting within a strictly defensive framework aimed ‌at protecting its ⁠interests, its regional partners, and freedom of navigation, and that it is ⁠unacceptable for our country to be targeted," Macron said in a post

item.title: EU to discuss bolstering Mideast naval mission amid Iran war turmoil
item.description: BRUSSELS, March 15 (Reuters) - European Union foreign ministers will discuss on Monday bolstering a small naval mission in the Middle East but are not expected to ‌decide on extending its role to the choked-off Strait of Hormuz, diplomats and officials say. The EU’s ‌Aspides mission was established in 2024 to protect ships from attacks by Yemen’s Houthi rebel group in the Red Sea.

item.title: North Korea holds Supreme People's Assembly election, KCNA reports
item.description: By Jack Kim and Heejin Kim SEOUL, March 16 (Reuters) - North Korea held an election for the Supreme People's Assembly on Sunday, state media KCNA said, to select delegates to the legislature that

item.title: Leftist, RN candidate virtually tied in first round of Marseille mayoral election, exit polls show
item.description: PARIS, March 15 () - Leftist incumbent ‌mayor Benoit ‌Payan is virtually ​tied with National Rally lawmaker Franck Allisio ‌in ⁠the first round of ⁠the mayoral election in ​Marseille, ​France's ​second-largest city, ‌two exit polls showed on Sunday. The far-right National Rally has ‌targeted Marseille ​as ​a ​test ‌of its strength ​ahead ​of the 2027 presidential ​election.

item.title: Filipinas seek abortions online in largely Catholic nation
item.description: Jane had been bleeding heavily for days before finally seeking help, not from a hospital but from the man who sold her the pills meant to end her six-week pregnancy.In January, a lawmaker filed a resolution calling for an investigation into the growing number of Filipinas resorting to social media for help in ending their pregnancies.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister rejects talk of dialogue with U.S. as strikes continue
item.description: Source: CBS News

item.title: Myanmar's post-coup parliament sits packed with junta allies
item.description: Source: AFP

item.title: Middle Eastern Headlines at 4:02 a.m. GMT
item.description: Trump says that he's asked 'about 7' countries to join coalition to police Iran's Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Asian Headlines at 4:37 a.m. GMT
item.description: Myanmar's parliament meets for first time in 5 years with military controlling most seats

item.title: Middle Eastern Business Headlines at 4:02 a.m. GMT
item.description: Trump says that he's asked 'about 7' countries to join coalition to police Iran's Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Asian Politics Headlines at 4:36 a.m. GMT
item.description: Trump suggests he may delay China trip as he pressures Beijing for help with Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Top Asian News 4:37 a.m. GMT
item.description: Myanmar opened its first parliamentary session in more than five years on Monday following an election that did not include major opposition parties, ensuring that the ruling military is set to retain a firm grasp on power. The military blocked Myanmar's last parliament from convening when it seized power from the last legitimately elected government of Aung San Suu Kyi in February 2021, and has governed without a legislature since then. It's touted elections held in December and January as a step toward the return of democracy.

item.title: Asian Business Headlines at 4:36 a.m. GMT
item.description: Trump suggests he may delay China trip as he pressures Beijing for help with Strait of Hormuz

item.title: At least 10 patients killed in hospital fire in India's Odisha
item.description: By Jatindra Dash BHUBANESWAR, India, March 16 (Reuters) - At least 10 people were killed in a fire that broke out in the trauma care unit of a hospital in the eastern Indian state of Odisha on Monday,

item.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
item.description: Here are the latest events in the Middle East war: - Flights temporarily suspended at Dubai airport - Flights were temporarily suspended Monday at Dubai's airport, previously one of the world's busiest, after a "drone-related incident" sparked a fire nearby, city authorities said. - Baghdad airport hit - Five people were wounded in a rocket attack on the Baghdad airport complex, which houses a US diplomatic facility, Iraqi authorities said.

item.title: Japan not planning Hormuz escort mission, PM Takaichi says
item.description: By Tim Kelly TOKYO, March 16 (Reuters) - Japan has no plan to dispatch naval vessels to escort vessels in the Middle East, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said on Monday, after U.S.

item.title: 20 Thai sailors return home after vessel attacked in Gulf
item.description: Twenty Thai crew members of a cargo ship that was attacked in the crucial Strait of Hormuz arrived in Thailand on Monday, with three of their colleagues still stranded on the vessel in the Gulf.Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had struck the Thai ship, as well as a Liberia-flagged vessel, in the strait because they had ignored "warnings".

item.title: Afghan asylum-seeker dies in ICE custody, US advocacy group says
item.description: By Joey Roulette March 15 (Reuters) - An Afghan immigrant who previously worked with the U.S. military in Afghanistan and later sought asylum in the United States died this weekend in Immigration and

item.title: Buzz, unease as UK crowds watch US bombers head to war
item.description: Hidden behind a tall hedgerow in the English countryside, Charlie Cumming stood at the fence of an air force base, camera raised towards a US B-1 Lancer bomber parked nearby.Seeing bombers loaded for active combat felt "quite surreal", Martin said.

item.title: Buried or not, Iran’s uranium a cause for concern, nuclear expert says
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said around 440 kilograms of 60%-enriched uranium is “under the rubbles."

item.title: What we know on the 16th day of the US and Israel’s war with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he is not yet ready to make a deal with Iran to end the war, and renewed calls for international allies to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Blackened, wrecked Russian tanker nears Malta
item.description: A liquified natural gas (LNG) carrier Russia claims was attacked by Ukraine in the Mediterranean is blackened by fire with two large holes in the hull, AFP footage showed Sunday.AFP footage taken from a plane showed it listing onto one side, parts of it blackened and seriously damaged by fire, with two holes either side in the middle of the hull.

item.title: Left leads in Paris, far right eyes gains in France local polls: projections
item.description: A Socialist candidate was leading in Paris while the far right looked strong in several southern cities as projections arrived from first-round local elections Sunday, seen as a political barometer ahead of France's presidential polls.In Paris, Socialist candidate Emmanuel Gregoire was in the lead ahead of former culture minister Rachida Dati, projections from two pollsters indicated.

item.title: NYC protesters call for regime change in Iran as conflict escalates
item.description: Organizers say the gatherings are meant to support the Iranian people and their fight for freedom and basic rights.

item.title: Israeli attacks kill 13 in Gaza, including 2 children and a pregnant woman
item.description: Meanwhile, more than 20,000 patients await evacuation as the Rafah crossing is set to partially reopen on Wednesday.

item.title: Israel launches fresh strike on south Beirut
item.description: Israel launched a fresh strike on Beirut's southern suburbs late Sunday as Israel's military said it was striking Hezbollah infrastructure in the city following earlier raids mainly in the country's south.Shortly after the raid, the Israeli military said on social media that it was "currently striking Hezbollah terror infrastructure in Beirut".

item.title: Iran’s ambassador to Saudi Arabia denies attacks on its oil facilities
item.description: Alireza Enayati says relations with Saudi Arabia are ‘progressing naturally’ and he’s in contact with Saudi officials.

item.title: Gen Z voters reject Iran war, politically divided twin brothers say
item.description: Twin brothers Nick and Nathan Roberts, who are on opposite sides of the political aisle, agree the conflict in Iran is hurting their generation.

item.title: Kazakhstan votes on constitution overhaul
item.description: Kazakhs voted Sunday in a constitutional referendum that authorities say will strengthen democracy -- though several proposed amendments appear rather to boost presidential powers in Central Asia's richest country.To boost turnout and support for the referendum, authorities have involved famous athletes and mobilised workers in mining and oil industries -- two important sectors in the Kazakh economy.

item.title: Pope Leo urges war leaders to halt fighting after deadly strike on school sparks outrage
item.description: Pope Leo XIV called Sunday for an immediate ceasefire in the war involving Iran, urgently urging world leaders to halt violence after deadly strikes hit schools and civilian areas.

item.title: Iran wants 'serious review' of Gulf ties, denies role in Saudi oil attacks
item.description: (Corrects in first paragraph to 'Gulf states' from 'Arab Gulf states') By Timour Azhari RIYADH, March 15 (Reuters) - Iran's relations with Gulf states will require a "serious review" in light of the

item.title: Ukraine eyes money and tech in return for Middle East drone support
item.description: Ukraine leader previously said advisers were sent to Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia to help thwart Iranian drone attacks.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon expected to hold talks soon, Israeli officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM, March 15 (Reuters) - Israel and Lebanon are expected to hold talks in the coming days aimed at securing a durable ceasefire that would see Hezbollah disarmed, two Israeli officials said on

item.title: Kazakhstan approves new constitution in referendum, exit polls say
item.description: By Felix Light, Mariya Gordeyeva and Tamara Vaal ALMATY/ASTANA, March 16 (Reuters) - Kazakh voters in a referendum on Sunday approved a new constitution that may provide President Kassym-Jomart

item.title: Two brothers investigated in France for allegedly planning antisemitic attack
item.description: The men were arrested after police found a semi-automatic firearm, a bottle of acid and an ISIS flag in their car during a roadside stop.

item.title: Former jihadist says sleeper cell attacks in US ‘very likely’
item.description: "If we're talking about real sleeper cells that are foreign-directed and constructed, they're very secretive and will leave the attack to speak for itself," Mubin Shaikh said.

item.title: Iran arrests 500 accused of giving information to enemies, police chief says
item.description: DUBAI, March 15 (Reuters) - Iran has arrested 500 people accused of sharing information with enemies, the Islamic Republic's police chief said on Sunday, as Israeli and U.S. fighter jets continue to

item.title: Iranian missile fragment hits US consul residence building in Israel
item.description: March 15 (Reuters) - A fragment of an Iranian missile struck a residential building used by the U.S. consul in Jerusalem, Israeli media reported, although the U.S.

item.title: Iranians cross into northern Iraq for cheaper food, internet and work after border reopens
item.description: Dozens of Iranians crossed into northern Iraq Sunday — the first day the border had opened since war struck their country — to buy cheaper groceries, access the internet, contact relatives and find work. Travelers said constant airstrikes and soaring food prices have made life in Iran increasingly desperate.

item.title: Hundreds join banned UK pro-Palestinian march
item.description: Hundreds of people turned out in London Sunday for a pro-Palestinian march banned by the government after police said it was organised by a group "supportive of the Iranian regime".The march was an "international demonstration... in support of Palestinians and all the oppressed around the world", the group added after the ban was announced.

item.title: Madagascar anti-corruption chief Rajaonarison named prime minister
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, March 15 (Reuters) - Madagascar's president Michael Randrianirina said on Sunday he had appointed anti-corruption chief Mamitiana Rajaonarison as prime minister, days after dismissing

item.title: Israeli forces shoot and kill 4 members of Palestinian family, officials say, after opening fire on car in West Bank
item.description: Four members of the same Palestinian family, including two boys aged five and seven, who were out on a late-night drive after breaking the daily Ramadan fast, have been shot dead by Israeli soldiers in the West Bank, Palestinian officials reported.

item.title: Iran arrests dozens accused of spying for Israel in new internal crackdown
item.description: Iran says it has arrested dozens of alleged spies linked to Israel across several provinces, accusing them of sharing the locations of military sites and economic infrastructure.

item.title: Congo Republic's Sassou seeks new term amid subdued turnout, internet outage
item.description: BRAZZAVILLE, March 15 (Reuters) - Congo Republic voted in a presidential poll on Sunday which is expected to extend the rule of President Denis Sassou Nguesso, one of Africa's longest-serving leaders,

item.title: Hungary pre-election showdown draws crowds amid foreign interference claims
item.description: Huge crowds joined rival marches staged by Hungarian premier Viktor Orban and his main opponent Peter Magyar in Budapest on Sunday, as both push allegations of foreign interference just four weeks before tightly-fought elections."We will not be a Ukrainian colony," read one of the banners carried ahead of the crowd, which later chanted "Viktor, Viktor" as the Hungarian premier took the stage in front of the parliament.

item.title: Why is NYC’s Mamdani facing criticism over response to attacks on wife?
item.description: Mayor’s wife criticised for past work related to Palestine, while Mamdani’s response rebuked by some supporters.

item.title: Germany sceptical about expanding EUOperation Aspides in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: FRANKFURT, March 15 (Reuters) - German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said on Sunday that he was sceptical about a potential widening of the European Union's Aspides naval mission to the Strait of

item.title: IDF says Michigan synagogue suspect's brother was Hezbollah commander
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said Ibrahim Muhammad Ghazali was responsible for managing weapons operations for the unit within the U.S.-designated terrorist group.

item.title: Hungary's feuding parties stage big rallies ahead of April election
item.description: By Krisztina Fenyo and Gergely Szakacs BUDAPEST, March 15 (Reuters) - Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban sought on Sunday to mobilise voters for what he called an "historic" vote on April 12, while

item.title: Fact Check: Slop Story About Permanent Home For Woman Working Part Time At 85 Is Bogus -- No Celebrity Donations
item.description: Did Keanu Reeves, Clint Black, Billy Joel, and various other celebrities suddenly all discover that a woman who once cared for them was still working part-time at 85 to pay her rent? No, that's not true: The sob story about...

item.title: In heavy rain, Lebanese fleeing war huddle under makeshift shelters
item.description: By Ahmed Kerdi BEIRUT/SIDON, March 15 (Reuters) - Hussain Murtada and his family are camping in the back of a small truck, a flimsy tarpaulin shielding them from a storm on Sunday, with no room left

item.title: No injuries after explosion at Jewish school in Amsterdam, officials say
item.description: Amsterdam's mayor said police have CCTV footage of a person placing the explosive device against the school's exterior wall.

item.title: Pope Leo decries 'atrocious violence' in Iran war, urges ceasefire
item.description: By Angelo Amante VATICAN CITY, March 15 (Reuters) - Pope Leo made an impassioned plea on Sunday for an immediate ceasefire in the expanding Iran war, lamenting "atrocious violence" that he said had

item.title: Iran says women's football captain withdraws Australia asylum bid
item.description: The captain of the Iranian women's football team which played in the Asian Cup in Australia has withdrawn her bid for asylum, state media said Sunday, making her the fifth member of the delegation to change her mind.Seven members of Iran's visiting football delegation competing in the Women's Asian Cup had sought sanctuary in Australia after they were branded "traitors" at home for refusing to sing the national anthem.

item.title: Kurds seek U.S. air cover to invade Iran
item.description: “When we cross the border, the United States should secure the skies for us and protect us from above,” said Rebaz Sharifi, a commander with the Kurdistan Freedom Party.
